verses.text: Hiram of Tyre, an old friend of David, sends to Solomon, who replies that David could not build the LORD's house because of his constant wars. The temple waits for a time of peace. There is a fitting season for every work of God, and He appoints the right hand for it.

Hiram of Tyre, an old friend of David, sends to Solomon, who replies that David could not build the LORD's house because of his constant wars. The temple waits for a time of peace. There is a fitting season for every work of God, and He appoints the right hand for it.

Hiram of Tyre, an old friend of David, sends to Solomon, who replies that David could not build the LORD's house because of his constant wars. The temple waits for a time of peace. There is a fitting season for every work of God, and He appoints the right hand for it.

Now, Solomon says, 'the LORD my God hath given me rest on every side,' so he will build the house God promised David's son would build. He asks Hiram for cedar and skilled woodcutters. Resting in God's peace, he turns at once to the work God appointed for that rest.

Now, Solomon says, 'the LORD my God hath given me rest on every side,' so he will build the house God promised David's son would build. He asks Hiram for cedar and skilled woodcutters. Resting in God's peace, he turns at once to the work God appointed for that rest.

Now, Solomon says, 'the LORD my God hath given me rest on every side,' so he will build the house God promised David's son would build. He asks Hiram for cedar and skilled woodcutters. Resting in God's peace, he turns at once to the work God appointed for that rest.

Hiram rejoices and blesses the LORD for giving David 'a wise son,' and agrees to float the timber down by sea. A Gentile king praises the God of Israel and lends his help. God draws even outsiders to honour His name and to serve the building of His house.

Hiram rejoices and blesses the LORD for giving David 'a wise son,' and agrees to float the timber down by sea. A Gentile king praises the God of Israel and lends his help. God draws even outsiders to honour His name and to serve the building of His house.

Hiram rejoices and blesses the LORD for giving David 'a wise son,' and agrees to float the timber down by sea. A Gentile king praises the God of Israel and lends his help. God draws even outsiders to honour His name and to serve the building of His house.

So Hiram supplies cedar and fir, Solomon supplies food, and the two make a treaty of peace; 'the LORD gave Solomon wisdom, as he promised.' God's gift of wisdom shows in wise alliances and fair dealing. True wisdom is practical, ordering even trade and treaties to good ends.

So Hiram supplies cedar and fir, Solomon supplies food, and the two make a treaty of peace; 'the LORD gave Solomon wisdom, as he promised.' God's gift of wisdom shows in wise alliances and fair dealing. True wisdom is practical, ordering even trade and treaties to good ends.

So Hiram supplies cedar and fir, Solomon supplies food, and the two make a treaty of peace; 'the LORD gave Solomon wisdom, as he promised.' God's gift of wisdom shows in wise alliances and fair dealing. True wisdom is practical, ordering even trade and treaties to good ends.

Solomon raises a great levy of labourers — tens of thousands hewing in the mountains and bearing burdens. The house of God is built by the ordered toil of many hands. No great work for God is done by a few; it takes a multitude, each bearing a share.

Solomon raises a great levy of labourers — tens of thousands hewing in the mountains and bearing burdens. The house of God is built by the ordered toil of many hands. No great work for God is done by a few; it takes a multitude, each bearing a share.

Solomon raises a great levy of labourers — tens of thousands hewing in the mountains and bearing burdens. The house of God is built by the ordered toil of many hands. No great work for God is done by a few; it takes a multitude, each bearing a share.

Thousands of overseers direct the work, and the builders prepare great, costly, hewn stones to lay the foundation. The base of God's house is laid with care and at cost. What is built for the LORD deserves a sure foundation and the best, most painstaking work we can give.
